programmes.show_description: How do they mine precious opals beneath one of the hottest deserts on earth? How do they build breath-taking stunt planes and how do they make nougat?
How do they farm the expensive spice saffron, how do they create giant roads made entirely from ice and how do they manufacture a muscle car?
Kate's Mini Countryman. Mike and Elvis are determined to buy a Mini Countryman for a caring mother. But the boys face a tough task with £500 to spend and no trade-in car.
Jordan's Subaru Impreza. The boys desperately want to buy policeman Jordan his dream car, a Subaru Impreza. Elvis surprises Mike as he dips into his contact book to buy a kit car.
Classic, Rare And Beautiful. In the West Country, Henry and Sam find some sheds full of classic bikes and vintage cars. They even find a limousine once owned by Eva Peron.
Yorkshires' Finest. Henry and Sam visit a fanatical biker in Yorkshire, who has the biggest collection of motorcycles they've seen so far. What bike will they come away with?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including Formula F race cars made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including vacuum excavators made?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A Mysterious Find. When four sisters need advice about what to do with their father's 1939 328 BMW, they call on Wayne. But importantly, will he be able to make them a profit?
What Wayne Wants. Wayne Carini welcomes viewers into the world of high-end car collecting. He must auction off a number of vehicles in his quest to obtain a 1911 Winton 17b.
Red Hot and Rowdy. Wayne Carini welcomes viewers into the world of high-end car collecting. A woman turns to Wayne for help in selling her late husband's 1965 Shelby Cobra 427 S/C.
Chance of a Lifetime. Wayne welcomes viewers into the world of high-end car collecting. He gets a personal look at the last remaining original 1967 Ferrari 275 GTB/4 N.A.R.T. Spyder.
Fine And Landy. Mike's found a British motoring icon: a 1976 Land Rover Series III. One of the few registered in California, it's a rare find but Ant has work to do to get it moving.
The British Are Leaving. Mike and Ant look back on their American adventure. Before they return home to the UK, they pack up the workshop and sort through the work of half a decade.
Try, Try, Triumph. To Ant's dismay, Mike buys a 1979 Triumph TR7. Underneath its disco-era styling are some quirks, like a homemade air cleaner, bad wiring and soft shocks.
TVR S2 - Part 1. Mike heads to Yorkshire to see a TVR S up for auction. Can he pull off the winning bid and get the car back in one piece for Edd to work his magic on?
TVR S2 - Part 2. Edd has been working hard to get a TVR S back on the starting blocks. But when more problems arise, will it be ready in time for Mike to sell for a profit?
Morgan. Mike is seduced by a stunning Morgan Plus 4 at only £13,000, but it needs a new chassis and fitting it will take at least a hundred man hours. How will Edd react?
TVR. Mike tracks down a TVR Cerbera for just £8,000. However, these classic sports cars are susceptible to rust. Can Edd patch it up or will the chassis need replacing?
Aston Martin DB7. Mike gets a great deal on a rundown Aston Martin DB7. Can Edd restore this classic at a profit - or will hidden problems blow their budget?
Dave Parkin. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave to acquire his Honda C90 and Gaz-69 Jeep. They're determined to revive both machines and return them to road-ready glory.
From Cornwall To Oxfordshire. Henry Cole and motorcycle guru Sam Lovegrove travel from Cornwall to Oxfordshire to rummage in some of Britain's most amazing sheds and make some cash.
Beach Buggy. In Leicestershire, Henry and Fuzz explore a shed full of classics, selecting a 1970s VW Beetle Beach Buggy. Can they restore it in time for a Somerset beach ride?
Mini. In Wiltshire, Henry and Fuzz admire Morris Minors and vintage mopeds. Can they revive a late 1980s Austin Mini in time for a historical road trip to Oxford?
Model A Ford. In awe of a huge collection of classic cars from all eras in Northamptonshire, Henry and Fuzz decide to transform a 1920s Model A Ford pickup truck.
American Dream. The Cogs bid for car-show glory with an iconic American pick-up truck. Richard is back on the racetrack. Can he impress the classic car power-players?
Metalwork expert Dominic restores an incredible 100-year-old banana barrow. Meanwhile, leather work expert Suzie repairs a well-used wallet with a remarkable history.
The team of restorers revive treasured heirlooms including a 1960s lawnmower, a Welsh rugby union trophy and a personalised leather belt.
Sacred Secret & Saucy Surprise. Angus sells the treasures of an Abbey to fund the monks' retirement, and a motoring fanatic's cheeky sculpture sets pulses racing on sale day.
Debts To Settle. The Blacklighters are pushing for a big pay day. With weather threatening to halt their operation, the Misfits have to get crafty with their setup.
The Bates. Paul Mackie and his expert team must haul a crew of first-time gold miners out of an epic three-year gold drought before their operation and family legacy ends in ruin.
Secrets Of Skull Grave. Archaeologists use state-of-the-art digital technology to uncover the mystery behind human skulls from Chile. What happened to these discarded skulls?
War Of The Ancients. Archaeologists use new digital technology to try and solve the mystery behind an ancient burial ground filled with bones, swords, an axe and more in Germany.
Everyday items go under the spotlight. How pharmaceutical blister packs, deli slicers and weather vanes are made.
Top and bowler hats, solar water heaters, sticky buns and electrostatic speakers go under the microscope.
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as electric boats and fencing foils made?
Follow the production process of aluminium foil, snowboards, soft polymer contact lenses and bread and find out how each of these items is made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes and fix them up for a profit. They turn trash into treasure!

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes and fix them up for a profit. They turn trash into treasure!

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes and fix them up for a profit. They turn trash into treasure!
Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer begin their hunt for antiques in Stow-on-the-Wold. Izzie buys a toast rack, while Charlie eyes a military first aid kit.
Izzie Balmer and Charlie Ross hunt for hidden gems in Gloucester. Plus, Izzie learns about the incredible story of Britain's first female astronomer.
Izzie Balmer and Charlie Ross continue their road trip in Hartley Wintney. Charlie buys a cut glass sugar shaker, while Izzie eyes three bronze pigs.
Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer search for interesting items in Salisbury. Plus, Izzie travels to Selborne to learn about one of the world's first ecologists.
Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer scour the Isle of Wight for profitable antiques. Plus, Charlie heads to Blackgang to explore Britain's oldest amusement park.
First up, the team bring a well-loved Indian guitar back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie is challenged with restoring a vintage RAF-issued flying jacket.
Silversmith Brenton is challenged with restoring a battered, old trophy. Plus, wood expert Will revives an impressive birdcage with a remarkable backstory.
Metal expert Dominic restores a 1940's snail riding toy. Plus, silversmith Brenton works on a treasured trophy won in a 1949 ballroom dancing competition.
Angus is in Newcastle to clear out a home that's full of books. He takes an interest in a Bechstein piano, a scary vase and a silver loving cup.
Sacred Secret & Saucy Surprise. Angus sells the treasures of an Abbey to fund the monks' retirement, and a motoring fanatic's cheeky sculpture sets pulses racing on sale day.
Susannah Constantine & Leslie Ash. Angus raids TV presenter Susannah's couture stash, then helps actress Leslie uncover treasures, memories, and a few famous surprises!
Metal expert Dominic gives a rare, old pedal aeroplane a new lease of life. Plus, luthier Julian works on a ukulele made from a box of cigars in 1926.
An ancestor of famous philosopher Simone de Beauvoir brings in a painting for a new lick of paint, and a beloved silver bracelet gets its charm back.
First up, the team bring a well-loved Indian guitar back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie is challenged with restoring a vintage RAF-issued flying jacket.
Silversmith Brenton is challenged with restoring a battered, old trophy. Plus, wood expert Will revives an impressive birdcage with a remarkable backstory.
